defs: In a different manner; in another way, or in other ways; differently; contrarily.
quotes: Thy father was a worthy prince, And merited, alas! a better fate; But Heaven thought otherwise.
It is said, truly, that the best men otherwise are not always the best in regard of society.

defs: In different circumstances; under other conditions; as, I am engaged, otherwise I would accept.
quotes: Let no man think me a fool; if otherwise, yet as a fool receive me.
Her eyebrows . . . rather full than otherwise.
notes: ☞ Otherwise, like so and thus, may be used as a substitute for the opposite of a previous adjective, noun, etc.
